connotation.def: 言外之意、內涵、含蓄的人物 (單數)
connotation.ex: The word has a strong negative connotation.

connoted.def: 隱含了的、內含的人的人 (或物)
connoted.ex: The connoted meaning was clear to experts.

connubial.def: 婚姻的、夫婦的人的人物
connubial.ex: They enjoyed ten years of connubial bliss today.

conquer.def: 征服、克服、戰勝的人的人
conquer.ex: He managed to conquer his fear of flying.

conquered.def: 被征服的、克服了的人的人 (或物)
conquered.ex: The conquered people were treated fairly.

conqueror.def: 征服者、勝利者人物 (單數)
conqueror.ex: William the Conqueror won the battle today.

conquest.def: 征服、佔領地、戰利品的人物 (單數)
conquest.ex: The Norman Conquest of England occurred.

consanguineous.def: 同血緣的、同胞的人的人物
consanguineous.ex: That was a rare consanguineous marriage.

consanguinity.ex: They shared a close local consanguinity.

conscience.col: clear conscience, guilty conscience, on one's conscience
conscience.ex: He has a very clear conscience about data.

conscienceless.ex: That was a cruel and conscienceless act.

conscientious.def: 盡責的、誠實的人的人物 (德)
conscientious.col: conscientious objector (拒絕服役者), conscientious hard work
conscientious.ex: He is a very conscientious and hard worker.

conscientiously.ex: She worked conscientiously on the project.

conscious.ex: The patient is now fully conscious and well.

consciously.ex: He chose his words very consciously today.

consciousness.ex: He began to lose consciousness after a fall.

conscript.def: 應徵士兵、徵召的人的人的人物 (單數)
conscript.ex: The young conscript was training for war.

conscription.ex: Military conscription is common in the city.

consecrate.def: 奉獻、使神聖的人的人人物 (單數)
consecrate.ex: The priest decided to consecrate a church.

consecrated.def: 神聖的、奉獻了的人的人 (或物)
consecrated.ex: The bodies were buried in consecrated ground.

consecration.ex: The mass consecration took place in June.

consecutive.def: 連續的、連貫的人的人物 (續)
consecutive.ex: They had three consecutive wins this week.

consecutively.ex: The pages were numbered consecutively now.

consensual.def: 一致同意的、合意的人的人物
consensual.ex: They reached a fair consensual agreement.

consensus.def: 共識、輿論、一致的人物 (單數)
consensus.ex: There is a general consensus on the topic.

consent.def: 同意、答應、認可的人的人人物 (單數)
consent.col: age of consent, silence means consent, mutually consent
consent.ex: They gave their consent to the new plan.

consenting.ex: They are both consenting adults tonight.

consequence.def: 後果、結果、重要性的人物 (單數)
consequence.col: of consequence (重要), face the consequences
consequence.ex: You must face the consequences of actions.

consequent.def: 隨後發生的、必然的結果的人的人物
consequent.ex: The flood and consequent loss was terrible.

consequential.def: 重要的、間接著的人的人物
consequential.ex: That was a highly consequential decision.

consequently.ex: He failed to work, and consequently failed.

conservation.def: 保護、保存、管理的人物 (單數)
conservation.ex: Energy conservation is vital for modern life.

conservationist.def: 自然保護者、環保人士人物 (單數)
conservationist.ex: The noted conservationist spoke about trees.

conservatism.ex: Political conservatism is strong in the city.

conservative.def: 保守的、守舊的、保守派的人物 (守)
conservative.ex: That was a very conservative estimate today.

conservatively.ex: She was dressed conservatively for the party.

conservatory.def: 溫室、音樂學院人物 (單數)
conservatory.ex: We had tea in the beautiful conservatory.

conserve.def: 保存、保護、果醬的人的人人物 (單數)
conserve.ex: We must try to conserve water in summer.

consider.def: 考慮、認為、體諒的人的人

considerable.ex: They have made considerable local progress.

considerate.ex: He is a very kind and considerate person.

considerately.ex: He acted considerately towards the pupils.

consideration.def: 考慮、體貼、酬金的人物 (單數)
consideration.col: under consideration, take into consideration, out of consideration for
consideration.ex: The plan is still under consideration now.

considering.ex: Considering the facts, he performed well.

consign.def: 交付、委託、寄發的人的人
consign.ex: She decided to consign the old books now.

consignee.def: 收貨人、受託人人物 (單數)
consignee.ex: The original consignee received the boxes.

consignment.def: 委託物、發貨、寄售的人物 (單數)
consignment.ex: A large consignment of grain has arrived.

consignor.def: 發貨人、委託人人物 (單數)
consignor.ex: The local consignor sent the cargo today.

consist.def: 由...組成、一致、存在於的人的人
consist.ex: Happiness does not consist in money now.

consistency.def: 一致性、稠度、凝聚的人物 (單數)
consistency.ex: The soup has a nice creamy consistency.

consistent.ex: His actions are not consistent with facts.

consistently.ex: She has behaved consistently for a year.

consolable.def: 可安慰的人的人物 (單數)
consolable.ex: The grieving man was not easily consolable.

consolation.def: 安慰、起安慰作用的人的人物 (單數)
consolation.col: consolation prize, few words of consolation
consolation.ex: He won a small consolation prize today.

consolatory.def: 安慰的人、慰藉的人的人物
consolatory.ex: She sent a very long consolatory message.

console.def: 安慰、儀表板、操縱台的人的人人物 (單數) (安)
console.ex: He tried to console his crying sister today.

consolidate.def: 鞏固、合併、加強的人的人
consolidate.ex: The leader tried to consolidate his power.

consolidation.ex: Debt consolidation helped him fix finance.

consonance.def: 一致、和諧、(樂)協和人物 (單數)
consonance.ex: They worked in full consonance together.

consonant.def: 輔音、子音、一致的的人的人物 (單數)
consonant.ex: The word starts with a voiced consonant.

consors.def: 配偶、夥伴、使結合的人的人人物 (單數)
consors.ex: The Prince Consort attended the opening.

consortium.def: 財團、聯盟、公會人物 (單數)
consortium.ex: The banking consortium funded the bridge.

conspicuous.ex: She was wearing a very conspicuous hat.
